title: Social Service Associate (Coach)
description:
Job Title: Social Service Associate (Coach)

Reports to: Supervisor

Chen Su Lan Methodist Children’s Home (CSLMCH) is dedicated to transforming the lives of children and families in need. Our mission is to foster healing, growth, and self-reliance within our community, emphasizing family reunification and improved psycho-social well-being.

Why Join Us:

  • Competitive pay: Motivating financial package and regular bonuses.
  • Competitive benefits and wellness package
  • Career growth and Development
  • Support Work Life Balance
  • And so much more

About the role:

We’re looking for passionate and dedicated individuals to join our team as Social Service Associate (Coach) in our Small Group Care (SGC) programme. At SGC, we provide trauma-informed care in a safe, nurturing environment to help Children and Young Persons (CYPs) heal, grow, and thrive. As a Social Service Associate (Coach), you’ll play a vital role in our clients’ daily lives and recovery journeys.

Responsibilities:

  • Build strong, nurturing relationships with and provide individualised support to 1–2 assigned children,
  • Help implement therapeutic plans and behavioural strategies (e.g., Positive Behavioural Plans, Occupational Therapy approaches),
  • Supervise daily routines, meals, school transitions, and structured activities,
  • Conduct weekly one-on-one therapeutic sessions,
  • Support behavioural modification and trauma recovery,
  • Collaborate with social workers, psychologists, and supervisors to track progress and plan interventions,
  • Communicate with stakeholders such as schools, families to assist with schoolwork and care arrangements,
  • Plan and run educational programmes to meet resident’s needs,
  • Support volunteer recruitment, scheduling, and external partnerships,
  • Complete documentation and ensure continuity of care between shifts

Work Schedule: 5-day work week (8am–6pm or 1pm–10pm); with one weekday off and Sunday rest day

Requirements:

Technical Competencies:

  • Minimum GCE “O” Level or equivalent
  • Proficient in English and a second language (Chinese, Tamil, Hindi, or Malay)

Core Competencies:

  • Experience working with children with trauma and behavioural challenges
  • Passionate about working with children
  • Patient, compassionate, and a strong team player
  • Positive and independent with excellent interpersonal skills
  • Committed to ethics, child safety, and long-term change

Preferred Achievements & Characteristics:

  • Able to supervise and manage small groups of children
  • Skilled in programme planning and activity execution
  • First-aid trained and able to respond to emergencies
  • Able to empathise and connect with children from diverse backgrounds

What are you waiting for:

Apply now and join us today with a dedicated team committed to making a difference in the lives of vulnerable children and families. You’ll have the opportunity to lead impactful initiatives, collaborate with passionate colleagues, and contribute to meaningful community-driven solutions. We work closely with community partners to bring holistic support and care to the children and families. Together we make a difference on the lives of the children in the Home. Together We Nurture Every Child and Family to Shine.

Only Shortlisted applicant will be notified.

Interested applicants may send their CV via this portal OR to hr@cslmch.org.sg. (You only need to choose one of these channels to submit your CV.)

postedCompany.description:
Mission

The Mission of the Home is to provide loving, Christian nurture for needy and disadvantaged children of all races and religions, so as to prepare them to be happy and responsible members of the family and community.

 

 

 

Vision

To be a premier Children’s Home and Centre where every child is nurtured to his/her full potential, ingrained with good Christian character to be happy and responsible members of their respective families and the community at large.

 

 

Goals

1.  To develop the Spiritual, Educational, Social, Physical and

Emotional well-being of the children.

2.  To provide an Inclusive Home environment to enhance the

integration of children with special needs and mainstream children.

3.  To assist parents in problem-solving and coping with their

difficulties.

4.  To strengthen Family relationships and enhance the Quality of

family life.
